title: UPS Battery Runtime Remaining
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where runtime_min < 10 OR (runtime_min < 20 AND load_pct > 70)
| table _time, ups_name, location, runtime_min, load_pct, battery_status
description: Minutes of runtime remaining under present load is the actionable metric for graceful shutdown sequencing during outages.
value: Minutes of runtime remaining under present load is the actionable metric for graceful shutdown sequencing during outages.
implementation: Poll `upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining` every 1ŌĆō5 minutes. Alert when runtime drops below site policy (e.g., <10 min). Correlate with concurrent generator tests.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP TA (UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining).
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="snmp:ups"` (runtime_min, load_pct).
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Poll `upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining` every 1ŌĆō5 minutes. Alert when runtime drops below site policy (e.g., <10 min). Correlate with concurrent generator tests.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:ups"
| where runtime_min < 10 OR (runtime_min < 20 AND load_pct > 70)
| table _time, ups_name, location, runtime_min, load_pct, battery_status
```

Understanding this SPL

**UPS Battery Runtime Remaining** ŌĆö Minutes of runtime remaining under present load is the actionable metric for graceful shutdown sequencing during outages.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="snmp:ups"` (runtime_min, load_pct). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP TA (UPS-MIB upsEstimatedMinutesRemaining). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: snmp:ups. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="snmp:ups". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where runtime_min < 10 OR (runtime_min < 20 AND load_pct > 70)` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó Pipeline stage (see **UPS Battery Runtime Remaining**): table _time, ups_name, location, runtime_min, load_pct, battery_status


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (runtime min per UPS), Line chart (runtime trend), Single value (minimum runtime in site).
visualization: Gauge (runtime min per UPS), Line chart (runtime trend), Single value (minimum runtime in site).
